food_subgroup: Two-layer steamed kuih: white compressed glutinous rice base (bottom) with green pandan coconut custard layer (top)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Kuih Seri Muka or Malay/Nyonya two-layer kuih. No defensible exact URI exists; closest parent would be a generic glutinous rice dessert node.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rice-based sweet snack / confection (closest FoodEx2 parent for glutinous rice dessert; no specific Malay kuih code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Kuih Seri Muka. A0FJP (rice-based confection) is the closest defensible parent. Specific Malay/Nyonya kuih are not represented in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Kuih Seri Muka or equivalent Malay kuih category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Kuih Seri Muka weighs approximately 80g and provides 180 kcal, 28.8g carbohydrate, 6.0g fat, and a glycemic load of approximately 22.5.

nutrient_source.title: Kuih Seri Muka — Health Promotion Board Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) for Kuih Seri Muka. HPB Singapore is an approved primary regional source for Singaporean dishes under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Glutinous Rice and Composite Kuih — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for glutinous rice (GI ~98) and egg/fat-moderated composite foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Kuih Seri Muka exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~78 is a composite estimate: the white glutinous rice base has a published GI of approximately 98–105 (University of Sydney GI Database, glutinous rice entries); the pandan custard top layer (eggs, coconut milk fat, rice flour) moderates the composite GI downward. Weighted composite estimate yields GI ~78 (high). Glycemic load per 80g serving = (78 × 28.8g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 22.5. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (80mg per 100g; approximately 64mg per 80g piece)
hypertension.guidance: Kuih Seri Muka is low in sodium and does not represent a primary dietary sodium concern for patients managing hypertension; however, total saturated fat intake from coconut milk (6.3g/100g) should be monitored in patients with concurrent cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~78) and high glycemic load (~22.5 per 80g piece) driven by white glutinous rice base (GI ~98–105)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit Kuih Seri Muka to one piece per occasion at most, as a single 80g piece delivers a glycemic load of approximately 22.5 — equivalent to the glycemic impact of more than one bowl of white rice — and three pieces would produce a cumulative GL of approximately 67.5; lower-GI Malay kuih alternatives such as Pulut Hitam or Bubur Cha Cha are preferable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk (6.3g/100g; approximately 5.0g per 80g piece) and dietary cholesterol from eggs (55mg/100g; approximately 44mg per piece)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of Kuih Seri Muka is predominantly from coconut milk medium-chain triglycerides, which are less atherogenic than long-chain animal saturated fats, but two pieces still deliver approximately 10g saturated fat from a single dessert serving; patients with established cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia should limit consumption to one piece and account for this within their daily saturated fat budget.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kuih Seri Muka (meaning 'beautiful face kuih' in Malay) is a classic two-layer Malay and Nyonya (Peranakan) steamed dessert that is one of the most visually distinctive traditional kuih in Singapore and Malaysia. The bottom layer is a dense, sticky, white compressed glutinous rice base cooked with coconut milk and a pinch of salt, pressed into a firm, chewy slab. The top layer is a smooth, vivid green pandan-scented custard made from fresh pandan juice, eggs, rice flour, coconut milk, and sugar, which sets to a silky, jelly-like consistency when steamed. The two layers are steamed together and served chilled, cut into rectangular or diamond-shaped pieces. Each standard piece weighs approximately 80g and delivers 180 kcal, 28.8g carbohydrate, 6.0g fat (5.0g saturated), and 3.2g protein. The white glutinous rice base has one of the highest glycemic indices of any common carbohydrate (GI ~98–105), making the composite kuih a high-GI food (GI ~78) with a glycemic load of approximately 22.5 per piece — a clinically significant glycemic event. The pandan custard layer moderates the GI slightly through its egg and fat content. Kuih Seri Muka is a staple at Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebrations, Malay kuih stalls, Peranakan restaurants, and community events across Singapore and Malaysia. It is sold at premium bakeries such as Bengawan Solo in Singapore. The food is vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Clinically, the 'small piece' appearance is deceptive: three pieces deliver a cumulative GL of approximately 67.5, equivalent to eating more than two bowls of white rice. Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should be specifically counselled about this kuih.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo), which is the approved primary regional source for Singaporean dishes under Project Veritas v2. The source match is exact for Kuih Seri Muka. GI is an analogous composite estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for glutinous rice, with confidence 0.9.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Kuih Seri Muka is not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB Singapore source for this item. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB Singapore source — no hydrogenated fats used in traditional preparation). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed negligible — pandan juice contributes trace amounts below detection threshold in the HPB source). All other micronutrient values are as reported by HPB Singapore Foodo. GI of ~78 is a composite analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Kuih Seri Muka exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Two portions are listed in the input (80g and 120g); the default serving used for GL calculation is 80g (1 piece). The 120g portion (2 pieces) would yield GL approximately 33.7. FoodEx2 code A0FJP assigned as closest defensible parent (rice-based confection); no specific Malay kuih code exists in FoodEx2.
